_comment: Stryker mutation-testing config — LOCAL ONLY, NOT in CI.
_run_comment: Run with: `npm run test:mutation`. Wall time ~30 min on a modern laptop.
_purpose_comment: Verifies the test suite isn't 'fake' — for each mutation injected into
src/services/latex/, at least one test must catch it. A surviving mutant
(no test failed) means the suite has a hole there.
Scoped to src/services/latex/ specifically: this is the highest-value /
highest-bug-density layer (the LaTeX generator, the flat-generator, the
escaper). Mutating the React components or Zustand stores would explode
Manual mutation testing during the property/fuzz audit confirmed:
- wrapTextForForm bypass was caught by 17 tests
- replacePlaceholders identity was caught by 6 tests
This config codifies the same check so future contributors can re-verify.
Verified locally with `npx stryker run --dryRunOnly`: 6 source files
matched, 2533 mutants instrumented, 201 tests run in the dry-run pass.
_thresholds_comment: in a full local Stryker run. The high/low/break values below are
set just under that so a fresh run doesn't false-fail; 'high' /
'low' remain aspirational targets to climb to as more property
tests strengthen. Bump these upward whenever a Stryker run shows
